scenario: Lattice Insurance points its Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot worker fleet at a SaaS cluster that handles 9000 jobs per minute. The team set camunda.client.mode to saas and now needs to identify which property under camunda.client.cloud carries the cluster identifier the unified client uses to compose its default connection endpoints.
question: Which camunda.client.cloud property carries the cluster identifier used when camunda.client.mode is saas in 8.8?

options.text: The properties reference documents camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to under the saas mode in 8.8.
The properties reference documents camunda.client.cloud.cluster-name as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to under the saas mode in 8.8.
The properties reference documents camunda.client.cloud.tenant-id as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to under the saas mode in 8.8.
The properties reference documents camunda.client.cloud.region as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to under the saas mode in 8.8.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.cloud section lists cluster-id as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to; it has type string with a null default.

b.text: Incorrect. cluster-name is not in the 8.8 camunda.client.cloud table; the documented key is cluster-id under that section.

c.text: Incorrect. tenant-id is not part of the camunda.client.cloud section; the cluster identifier is camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id in 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. The region property selects the geographic region; the cluster identifier is camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id in 8.8.

explanation: The camunda.client.cloud section of the 8.8 properties reference lists cluster-id as the cluster ID the Camunda client connects to when composing SaaS connection details from camunda.client.mode: saas.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
